_doc: Curated MERIT-station → fuel mappings. Checked BEFORE fuzzy matching in
gridscrapers/fuelmix.py: an entry here always wins and never lands in
plant_match_review. Keys: zone → exact MERIT PowerStationName.
Survives registry refreshes (keyed on MERIT names, not registry ids).
'registry' and 'note' are documentation only; 'fuel' is what the pipeline uses.
v2: top-30 review batch (2026-06-11). 29 of 30 confirmed coal (fallback was
already right for CI; entries added to claim the match + pin identity).

KHARGONE.note: NTPC Khargone STPP, 2x660 MW ultra-supercritical, Madhya Pradesh. Absent from GPPD (commissioned 2019-20). KAHALGAON candidate is a different NTPC plant in Bihar — correctly rejected.

SSTPS_II.note: Shree Singaji TPS Stage II (MPPGCL, Khandwa), 2x660 MW coal.

SSTPS_I.note: Shree Singaji TPS Stage I, 2x600 MW coal.

SGTPS -4X210.note: Sanjay Gandhi TPS Birsinghpur (MPPGCL), 4x210 MW units, coal.

SGTPS -1X500.note: Sanjay Gandhi TPS Birsinghpur unit 5, 500 MW, coal.

GTPS URAN (UNIT 05 TO 10).note: Mahagenco Uran CCGT 672 MW. Fuzzy score 0.63 was below threshold; coal fallback was wrong.

TROMBAY UNIT-5.note: Tata Trombay U5 is the 500 MW coal unit. GPPD's station-level 'TROMBAY (oil)' entry is unit-blind.

TROMBAY UNIT-6.note: U6 500 MW dual oil/gas; scheduled 0 MWh on sampled day. Revisit if it starts dispatching.

ADANI- TIRODA (UNIT- 1,4,5).note: Adani Power Tiroda 5x660 MW coal. Candidate 1 (TIRORA TPP, 0.56) is correct — spelling variance Tiroda/Tirora defeated the fuzzy threshold.

ADANI TIRODA (UNIT- 2,3).note: Same station as above, other units.

RATAN INDIA UNIT-01 TO 05.note: RattanIndia Power Amravati TPP, 5x270 MW coal.

KHAPERKHEDA UNIT - 01 TO 04.note: Mahagenco Khaperkheda TPS, 4x210 MW coal. Candidate 2 (K_KHEDA II) is the same station; candidate 1 (KHAMBERKHERA IPP) is not.

KRISHNAPATNAM (DSTPP) – I.note: Sri Damodaram Sanjeevaiah TPS (APPDCL Krishnapatnam) Stage I, 2x800 MW coal. Both fuzzy candidates are different plants. NOTE: key contains an en-dash (–), copied exactly from MERIT.

K.PTNAM U3.note: Krishnapatnam (SDSTPS) Unit 3, 800 MW Stage II, coal.

HINDUJA.note: Hinduja National Power, Visakhapatnam, 2x520 MW coal. UCHPINDA candidate (0.67) is a Chhattisgarh plant — wrong identity, right fuel.

THERMAL POWERTECH CORPORATION OF INDIA L.note: TPCIL / Sembcorp Energy India, Krishnapatnam, 2x660 MW coal.

VIJAYAWADA TPS (DR NARLA TATA RAO TPS (D.note: Dr. NTTPS Vijayawada (APGENCO) stages I-III, 6x210 MW coal.

DR.NTTPS STG-V.note: Dr. NTTPS Vijayawada Stage V, 800 MW, coal.

TELANGANA STPS STAGE-1.note: NTPC Telangana STPP (Ramagundam), 2x800 MW coal. Both gas candidates are wrong — the fuzzy matcher's trap row. Listed under AP allocation in MERIT.

CSTPP UNIT5.note: Chhabra STPP Unit 5, 660 MW supercritical (RVUNL/Adani), coal.

SSCTPSUNIT7_8.note: Suratgarh Super Critical TPS units 7-8, 2x660 MW (RVUNL), coal.

RAJWEST LTPS (IPP).note: JSW Barmer Rajwest Lignite TPS, 8x135 MW. LIGNITE — counted as coal per emission_factors (CEA fleet factor includes lignite); lignite-specific factor is a v2 refinement.

COASTAL ENERGEN.note: Coastal Energen Mutiara TPP, Tuticorin, 2x600 MW coal.

NCTPS STAGE 1.note: North Chennai TPS Stage 1 (TANGEDCO), 3x210 MW coal. DADRI candidate is NTPC in UP — wrong.

NCTPS STAGE 2.note: North Chennai TPS Stage 2, 2x600 MW coal.

JINDAL POWER LIMITED STAGE-2.note: Jindal Power Tamnar (Chhattisgarh) Stage II — TN's PPA share. Plant is physically out-of-state but fuel is coal.

CPP_BIOMASS_COGEN.note: Aggregate of captive biomass/bagasse cogeneration plants. Was falling back to coal (950g) — now biomass (230g). The one CI-changing fix in this batch.

WTPS 8.note: Wanakbori TPS unit 8, 800 MW supercritical, coal.

ESSAR POWER GUJ.note: Essar Power Gujarat = Salaya TPP, 2x600 MW COAL. The 0.67 gas candidate (ESSAR GT, Hazira) is a different Essar plant — confirmed trap, do not 'fix' this to gas.

RTPS.note: Most likely NPL Rajpura TPS, 2x700 MW; alternatively GGSSTP Ropar — both Punjab, both coal, so fuel is safe either way. Pin exact identity when Punjab SLDC plant names are cross-referenced.
